checklist.entry: Use the MCP authorization specification where HTTP-based transports and protected resources require authorization.
Validate token audience and do not use token passthrough patterns.
Use least-privilege scope design, progressive elevation and audit logging for scope changes.
Do not treat client-provided user identity as authoritative without server-side verification.
Users must understand and consent to data access and operations.
Hosts must obtain explicit user consent before exposing user data to servers.
Hosts must obtain explicit user consent before invoking tools.
Users should control whether sampling occurs and what result the server can see.
Local server configuration must show the exact command and require explicit approval before execution.
Sandbox local servers where possible and constrain filesystem, network and process privileges.
